council. The Review and certain
business interests have suggested,
urged and by other means tried to
force organized labor into this
fight.


believe that the agitation has
accomplished this, the awakening
of the unions to the point that they
will use the right backed by union
principle to take up this matter.


Mr.. Councilman-If all interests
had been noted in the franchise re-
lection you would not have had the
present dissension and organized
labor would have had no occasion
to complain.


ask you, Mr.. Councilman, in any
franchise granted, has there ever
been one item, ore clause, where
union conditions have been sug-
gested and lived up to? No, not
one.


Yes, Mr.. Councilman, organized
labor is going to enter this fight,
and backed by the first principle of
organized labor.


The successful union is based on
the good working condition of its
individual member, made possible
by union hours and union wages, in
their steady employment. The first
principle then of organized labor
is hours and labor; give these con-
ditions to the union man, and all
other questions will be taken care
of by the union men.


Then in taking up this question
let organized labor say to the Mil
waukee and North Coast railroads,
you submit to the severa! unions
of this city an agreement, granting
union conditions on all your work
in this city and you will receive
not from the Review, not from the
city council, but from the union
members of the organization,
backed by the principles OF organ
lied labor, the sentiment Of the
union men. GEO. w. MILLER.
